skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires fresh verification evidence before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, or ready for commit or review.
skill.summary: Require command output and evidence before any completion claim.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from documentation and evaluation text, not executable code. Backtick detections are Markdown fences or escaped examples, weak-crypto detections are ordinary prose or field names, and reconnaissance detections are verification guidance. No evidence found of command execution, data exfiltration, network access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines are Markdown code fences or escaped code-fence text inside documentation. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines were inspected and are documentation formatting or quoted examples. There is no executable source file, command runner, shell invocation, or user input path.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the referenced lines contain description fields or plain-language verification content. No hashing, encryption, certificate handling, or cryptographic API usage appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected content is prose and JSON metadata. I found no cryptographic implementation, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive key material.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Patterns Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged system and network reconnaissance patterns, but the referenced lines discuss checking agent reports, trust, and communication claims. No system enumeration, network scanning, host discovery, or information collection command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is guidance about verification discipline. I found no commands or network behavior that could perform reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: AI coding agents can report success before they have verified the work. This skill requires fresh command output and explicit evidence before any completion claim.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh verification before claims that work is complete, fixed, or passing.
Defines a gate for identifying, running, reading, and reporting verification output.
Separates test, linter, build, regression, and requirement verification claims.
Discourages vague status language such as should, probably, or seems to.
Requires independent checks before trusting delegated agent reports.
Promotes red-green verification for regression tests.
limitations: It does not choose project-specific verification commands automatically.
It does not execute commands without the agent taking action.
It cannot prove correctness when a project has weak or missing tests.
It relies on accurate reporting of command output and exit status.

use_cases.title: Prevent Premature Completion Claims
use_cases.description: Require evidence before an assistant says a bug is fixed or a task is done.

use_cases.title: Improve Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Make agents run and report relevant checks before commits, pushes, or pull requests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Verification
use_cases.description: Apply one verification rule across teams that use AI coding assistants.

prompt_templates.title: Verify A Simple Claim
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check before saying work is done.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before you claim this is complete, identify the command or check that proves it. Run it now, read the output, and report only what the evidence supports.

prompt_templates.title: Confirm Tests Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate check for test-related success claims.
prompt_templates.prompt: Do not say tests pass until you run the relevant test command in this turn. Report the command result, failure count, and final status.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Build And Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced check before release or pull request work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify the build and each stated requirement separately. Report which checks passed, which failed, and which could not be verified.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Delegated Agent Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced check when another agent reports success.
prompt_templates.prompt: Treat the delegated report as unverified. Inspect the actual changes, run the relevant checks, and report the verified state with evidence.

output_examples.input: The bug should be fixed now.
output: I cannot claim the bug is fixed from code changes alone.
I verified the original failure path with the project check.
The check passed, so the fix is supported by evidence.

output_examples.input: Are we ready to open the pull request?
output: I verified tests, build status, and the stated requirements.
All required checks passed in this turn.
The pull request is ready based on those results.

output_examples.input: The sub-agent says everything is done.
output: I treated the sub-agent report as unverified.
I inspected the actual changes and ran the relevant checks.
The verified state matches the requested work.

best_practices: Run verification fresh in the current turn before making any success claim.
Match each claim to the specific evidence that proves or disproves it.
State gaps clearly when checks are unavailable, partial, or failing.
anti_patterns: Claiming success because the code looks correct or the change was small.
Using a linter result as proof that tests or builds pass.
Trusting delegated agent reports without checking the actual work.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests by itself?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to run the relevant project checks before making claims.

faq.question: Can an agent claim success from a previous test run?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires fresh evidence from the current work context.

faq.question: What if no verification command exists?
faq.answer: The agent should say verification is unavailable and avoid unsupported completion claims.

faq.question: Does it apply outside software tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It applies to any claim that can be checked against evidence or requirements.

faq.question: How does it handle failing checks?
faq.answer: The agent should report the failure clearly and avoid saying the work is complete.

faq.question: Is this useful with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides a tool-neutral verification discipline for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
